rules.name: Brute Force Attack
rules.description: 5 or more failed login attempts detected in a 5-minute window
rules.explanation: Multiple failed login attempts detected. This is a likely brute-force or credential stuffing attack targeting authentication.
do_steps: Block the source IP address immediately
Review authentication logs for the affected asset
Notify the security team and OT engineer
Enable detailed logging on the affected device
Check for successful logins from the same source
dont_steps: Do NOT shut down the PLC without OT engineer approval
Do NOT delete logs as they are needed for investigation
Do NOT change passwords remotely without authorization

rules.name: Unauthorized PLC Modification
rules.description: Unauthorized change to PLC program or configuration detected
rules.explanation: An unauthorized modification to a PLC program was detected. This could indicate sabotage, ransomware, or insider threat targeting critical industrial systems.
do_steps: Alert OT engineer and security team immediately
Take a snapshot of current PLC state before any changes
Enable enhanced logging on the PLC and connected network
Document the unauthorized change for compliance
Initiate change verification procedure with OT engineer
dont_steps: Do NOT revert PLC program without OT engineer assessment
Do NOT shut down PLC without evaluating process impact
Do NOT allow further remote access until investigation complete
Do NOT assume this is a legitimate change without verification

rules.name: Malware Detected
rules.description: Host-based malware or malicious process detected on OT network
rules.explanation: Malicious software or process has been detected. This could spread to other systems and compromise OT network integrity.
do_steps: Alert security team and OT engineer immediately
Take forensic snapshot of affected system
Increase network monitoring for lateral movement
Identify all systems that communicated with infected host
Prepare network isolation plan pending engineer approval
dont_steps: Do NOT isolate network without OT engineer approval
Do NOT delete malware files without forensic snapshot
Do NOT run antivirus scans that could interfere with OT processes
Do NOT restart infected system without checking process safety

rules.name: Network Scanning / Reconnaissance
rules.description: Network scanning or reconnaissance activity detected from an internal or external host
rules.explanation: A system is scanning the network for open ports or services. This is a common first stage in cyber attacks targeting industrial systems.
do_steps: Block the scanning source IP
Review firewall logs for the scanning activity
Alert security team to investigate source
Enable enhanced network monitoring
Check if scan originated from inside or outside OT network
dont_steps: Do NOT assume internal scanning is safe without investigation
Do NOT ignore even low-severity scans in OT environments
Do NOT block critical control traffic while blocking scanner

rules.name: Firmware Modification
rules.description: Unauthorized firmware or configuration change detected on OT device
rules.explanation: Unauthorized firmware modification detected. This could indicate a supply chain attack or persistent threat actor targeting OT device integrity.
do_steps: Alert OT engineer and security team immediately
Take snapshot of firmware version and configuration
Enable detailed logging on all connected OT devices
Verify firmware hash against known-good baseline
Document all changes for compliance and investigation
dont_steps: Do NOT flash new firmware without verifying source integrity
Do NOT shut down device mid-operation without safety assessment
Do NOT allow remote firmware updates during investigation
